title: Aureline docs-pack manifest contract
description: Boundary schema for the docs_pack_manifest_record and the docs_pack_example_record frozen in docs/docs/docs_pack_manifest_contract.md. A docs-pack manifest is the governed artifact the docs_pack_registry, generated_reference_index, mirrored_docs_index, curated_knowledge_pack_registry, and support_runbook_registry (the canonical owners named in docs/adr/0013-docs-help-service-health-truth.md) publish so every docs pane, docs browser, Help / About footer, service-health row, support summary, onboarding step, and AI-explanation overlay can resolve a help_status_badge_record field-for-field without inventing badge semantics. Raw docs bytes, raw runbook bodies, raw symbol definitions, and raw URLs never cross this boundary; the manifest carries opaque ids, typed vocabulary, and signing / lineage / locale / example-label metadata only. The eventual docs-help-service-health crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new source class, publisher class, signature-status state, mirror-chain-status value, acquired-via value, locale-coverage class, example-label class, stale-example reason, citation-posture value, backlink-posture value, publishable-blocking reason, version-match state, freshness class, client scope, redaction class, or policy-context field is additive-minor and requires a docs_pack_manifest_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

docs_pack_manifest_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this docs-pack manifest export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / claim manifests. Raw URLs, raw page bodies, raw runbook bodies, and raw symbol definitions never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

bcp47_locale_tag.description: BCP-47 language tag (e.g. en, en-US, pt-BR, zh-Hant-HK). Case is normalized by the registry but preserved here for round-trip compatibility.

source_class.description: Frozen subset of the ADR-0013 source-class vocabulary that is admissible for a docs-pack manifest. Vendor / provider docs ride the connected-provider record (ADR-0010) and are not shipped as docs packs; derived_explanation rows are session-scoped, not packaged; external_status_feed rows are live feeds, not packs.

publisher_class.description: Frozen publisher-class vocabulary. first_party_project is the running project itself; permitted_publisher is a third-party publisher the project has allow-listed; official_upstream_mirror is a signed mirror of an upstream canonical source; operator_curated is a pack an operator (admin / ops team) assembled for a deployment; support_pipeline is a support-runbook pack pinned by the support-export pipeline.

signature_status.description: Frozen signature-status vocabulary. signed_and_verified is the only state that clears the signature gate; every other state is a publishable blocker and surfaces as the signature_unverified degraded-state cause on the help_status_badge_record.

mirror_chain_status.description: Frozen mirror-chain-status vocabulary. not_applicable is used for source classes that do not mirror an upstream (project_docs, generated_reference, support_runbook). continuous means every predecessor digest resolves and the signing chain verifies end-to-end. predecessor_missing and signing_chain_broken surface as the mirror_continuity_broken degraded-state cause and are publishable blockers.

acquired_via.description: How this pack revision reached the running instance. online_fetch is a live fetch from the canonical owner. signed_bundle_import is an out-of-band signed bundle (e.g. enterprise distribution). air_gapped_media is an offline import from physical media. local_build_output is a pack built alongside the binary (typical for project_docs / generated_reference). host_process_internal covers packs that the host process ships internally and never persists to disk.

locale_coverage_class.description: Per-locale coverage class. complete means every authoritative page in the primary locale has a reviewed translation. partial means some pages are missing translations and will fall back to the primary locale with a typed disclosure. machine_assisted means translations exist but were produced by a machine-translation pipeline without human review and MUST render a typed disclosure. stub means only page titles / navigation exist and body text falls back. stale_copy means the translation was reviewed but against a prior revision of the source; surfaces MUST render a stale-translation disclosure on the primary surface.

example_label_class.description: Frozen example-label vocabulary. stable_example is the publishable default. stale_example means the example references a symbol, setting, command, or API surface that has moved (deprecated, renamed, removed, or whose behavior diverged); surfaces MUST render the stale disclosure on the primary surface (tooltip-only disclosure is forbidden). needs_review_example flags examples whose target moved but whose renderability has not been re-verified. quarantined_example is hidden from docs panes and flagged to the publisher; it still ships in the manifest so parity audits can count it.

stale_example_reason.description: Frozen stale-example reason vocabulary. Every stale_example / needs_review_example / quarantined_example label carries exactly one reason so parity audits and support exports can enumerate why a pack degraded without free-form strings. Adding a reason is additive-minor and bumps the schema version.

citation_posture.description: Pack-level citation posture consumed by the ADR-0013 derived_explanation rule. citation_required means every authoritative claim drawn from this pack MUST carry at least one citation_anchor_record when it backs a user-visible explanation (derived_explanation and support_runbook packs typically sit here). citation_recommended means anchors SHOULD be attached but are not a publishable blocker. citation_not_required is admissible for generated_reference packs whose anchors are the reference itself.

backlink_posture.description: Whether anchor ids inside this pack resolve back to the canonical owner on demand. backlink_resolvable means the canonical owner is online and every anchor round-trips. backlink_deferred means anchors are valid but resolve against an offline / cached owner until the next refresh window; parity audits treat deferred backlinks as valid but lower freshness. backlink_not_required is admissible for self-contained reference packs (generated_reference) whose anchors target the pack itself.

version_match_state.description: Re-exported from schemas/docs/help_status_badge.schema.json without modification. Computed against the running build's exact-build identity at render time. When the pack is newer than the running client (pack target build is above the client's compat ceiling), the state is incompatible_drift_detected and the pack MUST render a typed degraded-state cause with a repair hook (upgrade_release_channel or refresh_freshness).

freshness_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json and schemas/docs/help_status_badge.schema.json without modification (ADR-0011 / ADR-0013). A manifest whose last_refreshed_at is outside the refresh_window MUST NOT declare authoritative_live.

client_scope.description: Re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json and schemas/docs/help_status_badge.schema.json without modification. A pack whose client_scopes excludes the rendering surface MUST NOT render as available there; the surface projects the client_scope_excludes_surface degraded-state cause instead.

redaction_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json without modification (ADR-0011 / ADR-0007). Declared per-manifest; the broker-owned redaction pass runs before bytes reach any persistent or exportable sink.

publishable_state.description: publishable is the only state the registry renders to docs panes / docs browser / Help / About / service-health / support surfaces. draft is admissible during authoring and does not resolve on protected surfaces. blocked means at least one publishable_blocking_reason applies. withdrawn is used when a newer revision has superseded this one; withdrawn manifests still resolve for support bundles and evidence packets. quarantined means the publishing pipeline or admin policy has quarantined the pack; surfaces deny render with a typed pack_quarantined cause.

publishable_blocking_reason.description: Frozen publishable-blocking-reason vocabulary. Every reason maps to a degraded-state cause on the help_status_badge_record that surfaces consume. A manifest with publishable_state = blocked MUST name at least one reason; a manifest with publishable_state = publishable MUST have an empty blocking-reasons list.

trust_state.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state at the moment the manifest was minted.

execution_context_id.description: ADR-0009 execution-context id when the manifest was minted inside a live execution. Null when minted outside an execution context.

policy_context.description: Policy context the manifest was minted under. ADR-0008 / ADR-0009 policy epoch and ADR-0001 trust state are re-validated on every render.

repair_hook_ref.description: Re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json without modification (ADR-0011). Used on manifests whose publishable_state is blocked to name the hook the registry surfaces alongside the typed blocking reason.

reviewed_against_source_revision_ref.description: Opaque pin to the source revision the translation was reviewed against. When set and not equal to the pack's pack_revision_ref, the row is a stale_copy candidate.

page_count_covered.description: Number of authoritative pages the translation covers in this locale. 0 is admissible for stub coverage.

page_count_total.description: Total number of authoritative pages in the primary locale the translation aims to cover.

locale_row.description: Per-locale coverage row. A manifest MAY carry one locale_row per available_locale so parity audits and docs search can surface coverage honestly on mixed-locale packs.

mirror_of_pack_id.description: Opaque id of the upstream pack this manifest mirrors. Required (non-null) when source_class = mirrored_official_docs; null otherwise.

upstream_revision_ref.description: Opaque pin to the upstream revision this mirror corresponds to.

predecessor_pack_revision_ref.description: Opaque pin to this mirror's previous revision. Required (non-null) for mirrors with mirror_chain_status = continuous; null for the initial mirror revision.

acquired_via.description: How this revision reached the running instance. Null only when mirror_chain_status = not_applicable.

acquired_at.description: When the revision was acquired by the running instance (last online fetch, last bundle import, last media import). Null only when mirror_chain_status = not_applicable.

offline_expiration_at.description: Monotonic deadline after which an offline / air-gapped acquisition MUST be treated as stale regardless of freshness_class. Null for packs acquired_via online_fetch / local_build_output / host_process_internal.

air_gapped_origin_label.description: Short operator-supplied label for the physical / out-of-band origin of an air-gapped import (e.g. 'enterprise distribution 2026 Q2'). Never contains raw URLs or raw file paths.

mirror_lineage.description: Offline / mirrored lineage block. Required on every manifest; packs that do not mirror upstream set mirror_chain_status = not_applicable and leave the other fields null. Mirror_continuity_broken / predecessor_missing / signing_chain_broken values map to the ADR-0013 mirror_continuity_broken degraded-state cause.

signing_chain_digest.description: Opaque digest identifying the signing chain. Required (non-null) for signature_status = signed_and_verified and signed_but_unverified; null for signature_missing and signature_revoked.

signing_authority_ref.description: Opaque pin to the signing authority record the registry used to verify this pack.

signing_block.description: Signing metadata for the pack. signature_missing and signature_revoked are publishable blockers and surface the signature_unverified degraded-state cause.

label_class.description: Fixed on stable_example_ref entries. stale / needs_review / quarantined entries ride the stale_example_ref shape.

target_symbol_or_anchor_ref.description: Opaque pin to the symbol / anchor the example targets. Lets later citation / symbol-reference packets cross-check that the example's target still resolves.

stable_example_ref.description: Lightweight reference to a stable example embedded in this pack.

label_class.description: Non-stable example posture. Surfaces MUST render the typed disclosure on the primary surface; tooltip-only disclosure is forbidden.

target_symbol_or_anchor_ref.description: Opaque pin to the symbol / anchor the example targets. Null when the target has been removed entirely.

superseding_example_id.description: Opaque pin to a replacement example (within this or a later pack revision), when one exists. Used by the typed disclosure to offer migrate_to_replacement on the repair hook.

stale_example_ref.description: Reference to a non-stable example embedded in this pack.

stable.description: All examples the pack certifies as stable at this revision.

stale.description: All examples the pack certifies as stale at this revision. Each entry carries a typed stale_reason and, when available, a superseding_example_id.

needs_review.description: Examples whose target moved but whose renderability has not been re-verified. Surfaces render them with a typed disclosure until a reviewer relabels them stable or stale.

quarantined.description: Examples the publisher or admin policy has quarantined. Hidden from docs panes; still enumerated here so parity audits can count them.

stale_examples_exceed_threshold.description: True when the ratio of (stale + needs_review + quarantined) to total examples exceeds the publisher-configured threshold. When true, publishable_state MUST NOT be publishable; the pack is either blocked with stale_examples_exceed_threshold or moved to draft / withdrawn.

example_summary.description: Pack-level example summary. The stable / stale split is the contract surfaces consume so docs panes can render stale-example disclosures on the primary surface and parity audits can compute the pack's effective freshness without parsing prose.

display_version.description: Human-legible version string surfaces render alongside the source / freshness chips (e.g. '2026.04.19-stable', 'mirror 2026-Q2', 'rev-14').

semver_version.description: Machine-readable semver version. The compat window (compat_window_semver) is computed against this value.

pre_release.description: True when the pack is a pre-release / nightly pack whose verification has not completed. Pairs with version_match_state = pre_release_unverified on the help_status_badge_record.

target_running_build_identity_ref.description: Opaque pin to the running build identity (D-0011) the pack was published against. Surfaces compute version_match_state against this id at render time.

compat_window_semver.description: Declared compat window the pack's content is authoritative for. Null only when source_class = generated_reference and the pack pins exactly to one build identity. A running build outside the window produces version_match_state = incompatible_drift_detected; 'pack is newer than the local client' is a special case of this.

available_locales.description: All BCP-47 locale tags the pack ships content for. MUST include primary_locale as its first element or somewhere in the set.

locale_coverage.description: Per-locale coverage rows. Non-empty for mixed-locale packs; MAY be empty when primary_locale is the only locale and available_locales has one entry.

refresh_window_seconds.description: Refresh window (seconds) the registry uses to decide whether the pack may continue to declare authoritative_live / warm_cached. 0 means 'refresh every render' (typical for generated_reference and host_process_internal packs).

last_refreshed_at.description: Monotonic timestamp of the last refresh against the canonical owner. Null for packs acquired_via host_process_internal / local_build_output that do not refresh independently of the binary.

client_scopes.description: Client classes this pack surfaces on. MUST be non-empty; a pack whose client_scopes would be empty is blocked with client_scope_empty.

required_citation_anchor_kinds.description: Anchor-kind vocabulary (subset of ADR-0013) this pack requires surfaces to attach when citation_posture = citation_required. Empty when citation_posture is citation_recommended or citation_not_required.

publishable_blocking_reasons.description: Frozen list of blocking reasons. MUST be empty when publishable_state = publishable; MUST be non-empty when publishable_state = blocked; MAY be empty for draft / withdrawn / quarantined (quarantined carries pack_quarantined implicitly).

repair_hook_ref.description: Required (non-null) when publishable_state = blocked. Null otherwise. Reuses the ADR-0011 repair-hook vocabulary.

supersedes_pack_revision_ref.description: Opaque pin to the prior pack revision this manifest supersedes. Null for the initial revision. Used for supersession / withdrawn rendering.

allOf.description: publishable_blocking_reasons MUST be empty when publishable_state = publishable.
publishable_blocking_reasons MUST be non-empty when publishable_state = blocked, and a repair_hook_ref MUST be present.
Mirror lineage fields are required when source_class = mirrored_official_docs.
Non-mirror source classes MUST declare mirror_chain_status = not_applicable.
Packs with citation_posture = citation_required MUST declare at least one required_citation_anchor_kinds entry.

docs_pack_manifest_record.description: Docs-pack manifest published by a canonical owner (docs_pack_registry, generated_reference_index, mirrored_docs_index, curated_knowledge_pack_registry, or support_runbook_registry). A surface MUST resolve this manifest before rendering a help_status_badge_record whose source_revision_ref points into the pack.

stale_reason.description: Required (non-null) when label_class is stale_example / needs_review_example / quarantined_example; null when label_class = stable_example.

locale.description: Locale the example is authored in. Null when the example is locale-neutral (e.g. a code sample with no prose).

allOf.description: stale_reason MUST be non-null when label_class is not stable_example.

docs_pack_example_record.description: Standalone record describing a single example a pack ships. Useful for parity audits and support exports that need to enumerate examples across packs without holding a full manifest.
